Module or functional area of the system (e.g., 策略 means policy); aligns logs to the same subsystem.
Action performed (e.g., 添加 means add); links events to specific operations for activity tracking.
Identifier of the policy being modified or applied; groups related policy changes.
Numeric identifier for the specific rule or log entry; enables exact event matching.
Source address, possibly empty, for network rules; used in session-based correlation.
Destination address, possibly empty, for network rules; used in session-based correlation.
Input interface of the network traffic or rule (e.g., any matches all); aids in network topology alignment.
Output interface of the network traffic or rule; aids in network topology alignment.
Service or application protocol associated with the rule; helps align events by network service.
Setting for whether logging is enabled (e.g., 关闭 means off); indicates monitoring state.
Status indicating if the rule is enabled (e.g., 开启 means on); used for rule life-cycle tracking.
User account that performed or owns the rule; critical for user-to-activity alignment.
Human-readable descriptive message explaining the rule action; allows for semantic grouping of similar events.
添加了类型为伪装的规则
Outcome status of the operation (e.g., 成功 means success); used for success/failure correlation.
Firewall log identifier, typically a numeric flag; links related firewall log entries.
